{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''[Dakerno]'' witnessed the ritualistic tribal customs of two warring factions—the Farangs and the Waroots. Impressed by the natives' centuries-old tactics, Dakerno created the holo game.|Gundark's Gear Datalog|<ref name="Gundark">''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]''</ref>}}
The [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] '''Farangs''' evolved on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ab'Bshingh]] alongside another intelligent [[Species/Legends|species]], the [[Waroot]]. The two species were at [[Ab'Bshingh civil war|war]] for [[Time/Legends|centuries]], resulting in the development of unique tactics and customs. [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] strategist [[Crat Dakerno]] studied these customs and based his [[holo boardgame]] [[B'shingh]] on them.

==History==
[[File:Bshingh-RidersOfTheMaelstrom.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A game of B'shingh featuring Waroot and Farangs]]
The Farangs were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that evolved on [[Ab'Bshingh]],<ref name="SWAJ" /> a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories's]] [[Sujimis sector/Legends|Sujimis sector]].<ref name="Atlas">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> For [[Time/Legends|centuries]], the Farangs [[Ab'Bshingh civil war|fought]] against a second species native to the planet, the [[Waroot]]. The conflict led to the development of unique ritualistic tribal customs and tactics over the many years of conflict. Eventually, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] intervened and dispatched forces to quell the violence on the planet's surface.<ref name="SWAJ" />

Among the [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] accompanying the Imperial [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] was military strategist [[Crat Dakerno]]. Impressed by the natives' tactics, Dakerno studied their customs, eventually creating the [[holo boardgame]] [[B'shingh]] from what he had learned. The game became popular in certain intellectual and military circles. The game involved moving Farangs and Waroot around the board in an attempt to capture the opponent's base. Since proficiency in the game required knowledge of the ancient customs of the Farangs and their opponents, the game had only limited popularity.<ref name="SWAJ" /> A B'shingh hologame was manufactured by [[Dakerno Inc.|Dakerno Incorporated]] and retailed for 500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="Gundark" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|I told Charlene to write a story involving this scene so I could feature the color artwork in the Journal. She went to work and submitted "A Certain Point of View," in the plot of which she managed to highlight several elements of the painting.|Peter M. Schweighofer|<ref name="Introduction">''[[Tales from the Empire]]'', "Introduction: A Galaxy Filled with Stories"</ref>}}
The Farangs were created by [[Charlene Newcomb]] for her short story [[A Certain Point of View (short story)|''A Certain Point of View'']], published in [[West End Games]]' [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 8|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 8]] in [[1995]]. Following a challenge from editor [[Peter M. Schweighofer]], Newcomb wrote this story based on the cover image of the [[1989]] adventure [[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']].<ref name="Introduction" /> This cover, illustrated by [[Daniel Horne]], contained a holographic board game, which was incorporated by Newcomb in the form of the Waroot and Farangs. While several figures can be discerned in the cover image, there has been no [[canon]] determination as to which figure belongs to which species.

A portion of the information from ''A Certain Point of View'' was later republished in the sourcebook ''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]'' in [[1997]]; the story itself was republished in ''[[Tales from the Empire]]'' in [[1997]] and on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] in [[2006]]. The Farangs also received an entry in [[2009]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
